meaning: n.狭长三角旗,燕尾旗,旗帜
examples: A small pennon borne on a lance.
He has had his lance stuck up with a pennon, which streams in a strong east wind.
The OED says "couch potato" originated as American slang, meaning "a pennon who spends leisure –time passively or idly sitting around, especially watching television or video tapes" .
Meanwhile, Pennon's results were boosted by bigger bills and a strong performance at its Viridor Waste unit.
Last year Ofwat told Pennon it could raise household bills for its South West Water customers by 25% in real terms over five years.
